notes: Game crashes very often either after intro where companies involved into the game development are listed or in the main menu or after game scenes. If, eventually, it did not crash (like 1/20 times) and I am in the game, there are black-pixel flickering in the game-scenes and when it comes to playing (you are free to use controls), the screen gets filled with black color in a minute, probably this is caused by motion blur. The FPS counter was good for "high quality" preset though, so, if the game does not have these glitches and crashes, it will run smoothly and become playable.

title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes:
Seems to need noesync to fix the crash/freeze before showing the main menu. Other than that the game runs very well with high graphics setting and exclusive fullscreen.

Seems to run much smoother in proton 3.16-8 than in 3.7-8
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes:
I had issues with black spots on Lara's skin. updating the graphics driver to Nvidia driver 418.xx series fixed this issue. Unfortunately I also had no choice but to upgrade to Ubuntu 18.04 ( despite the statics of my system on here says its 16.04. It is actually 18.04lts) since canonical team hadn't included driver 418 for ubuntu 16.04 and also cause I kept getting Login loop error every time I tried to install driver 418. 
I'm giving this a silver cause I experienced frame rate slowdowns occasionally and pressing Q to show hidden objects would make the bushes / foliage flicker pink for a few seconds. Overall though the game is still playable thank god.

title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: update on my previous post regarding upgrading from 16.04lts to 18.04 because of screen login loop as well as needed to upgrade nvidia drivers. so basically I've managed to eliminate the issue that some have had regarding frame stuttering in the game. I was experiencing this regularly until I found online query that when you launch the game, open system monitor and look for SOTTR.EXE and right click it and change the priority  from 'normal '  to  'very high'.  i played for 5 hours plus last night and experienced no stutter in frames. chris33556
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes:

Other than that the game runs very well with high graphics setting and exclusive fullscreen. Steam controller works fine. It does show a warning that no graphics adapter is installed at startup but that can be ignored.
I have tried using the Wine D3D11 tweak like others suggested but for me that just makes the whole game so slow it is unplayable.

title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: I've tested this game on 3 different PCs. SOTTR only runs on an RTX card it seems. 2 systems I have running on GTX 1070 (with Ryzen 5 2600, on Manjaro) and GTX 1060 (with i3 8100 on Ubuntu Mate) have broken graphics and massive stuttering making them unplayable, but on RTX 2080 (with i7 7700 on Ubuntu Mate) it runs without problem. I don't have any AMD GPU so I can't comment on that.

notes: Game launches, but framerate stutter was sickeningly bad when loading a save and starting a new game. DLSS is greyed out in the launcher settings. I say wait for Feral's port.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes:
Playable on Highest settings framerate not as good as on Windows, you lose about 25% performance and sometimes some stutter. On windows the benchmark gives me 100% gpu bound, on Linux only 11%. The game seems to be more CPU-hungry on Linux for rendering frames.
I also experience the "Black paint effect", the screen fills up with black en becomes unplayable. The menu's remain visible though so you can restart the game from your last save.  Apart from that I haven't seen any graphical glitches.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes:
Ran the game without much issues on custom settings at 1080p (pretty much very high detail, with motion blur switched off). No issues at all, although there are the occasional stutters when the game is saving/loading. Running the benchmark was a bit of an odd one, because as smooth as some sections were, the part in the vilage was really, really choppy.

Overall, I'd say it's playable on the right hardware, though I'd give it some more time to see if feral is going to release a native version for Linux as they've done with the previous titles. The moment they do, I'd recommend anyone to buy it. It's not the absolute gem of the series since the 2013 reboot, but it's a AAA title that has had consistent Linux support and I, for one, believe the devs deserve to be rewarded for that. All the same, the game is good, I'd say about on par with the 2013 Tomb Raider, but with a few more spacial puzzle elements that are actually reminiscent of the old tomb raider series.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes:
With proton 4.2 I was able to run the game without any tweaks, but I did have to increase the hard nofile limit as described at https://github.com/zfigura/wine/blob/esync/README.esync  .

I was playing with a steam controller and the performance was good most of the time but the input would occasionally feel laggy, especially on the last fight which I ended up using the keyboard and mouse for.

Also after every death and reload from checkpoint the game performance would decrease. After about 4 death's the fps would be so low it was unplayable so I would have to quit and restart the whole game. This was the only really annoying problem for me.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: poor performance
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: Use internal DXVK resulted in severe CPU overhead, only 1-2 cores are 100% working. I compiled the latest version (1.3.1) of DXVK from GitHub (https://github.com/doitsujin/dxvk/releases), using MinGW-w64-GCC provided by the "ownstuff" archlinux unofficial repository. The DXVK 1.3.1 worked nicely, all 6 CPU cores are working at ~80% and no obvious stuttering or lag was noticed.

title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes:
Ran the trial with ACO. 
If you REALLY want to play it, you can absolutely do so. Runs out of the box and the performance is (outside of parts with many people) pretty good actually. 

BUT: There is a noticeable stuttering going on. Not constantly, but every now and then. Cutscenes can get choppy at times. Sound in these can(!) be async as well. In areas where many people run around, the framerates tank heavily (down to ~20 fps). 

Playable? Absolutely, but you'll notice some minor issues -> only silver rating
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: The game runs without any major problems. Sometimes the game stutters when reloading.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: Add -nolauncher to skip the launcher. It says it doesn't detect device but runs fine. Disable Steam overlay to avoid mouse issues with alt+tab. Stutters when caching shaders but is not game breaking. Great performance with fsync. Getting 60~100fps in ultra.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: After upgrading to 3rd gen Ryzen, the game does not start anymore.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: After adding the parameter clearcpuid=514 to the kernel boot options, it worked, with ACO I am seeing some artifacts, however it is running very smoothly
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: Version 4.11-1 actually corrected 2 things from the last time I tested this game. First the performance is much better. Previously there was lag in the main menu, during the intro video and even when appearing for the first time in a new zone. All of this is gone and the game runs very smooth. I could not find any difference between FPS on Windows 10 and Ubuntu. So Proton 4.11-1 actually made a huge difference here.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: The game runs almost flawlessly. I didn't have to make any tweaks to get this game up and running, runs great out of the box. The only con that I have is for some studdering that happens from time to time which I'm guessing has to do with preshader caching.  Other then that I have settings on high with v-sync enabled and I'm getting a pretty steady 60 FPS.

title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes:
I find this game works much better with AMDVLK, performance wise.

VK_ICD_FILENAMES="/usr/share/vulkan/icd.d/amd_icd64.json" %command%

(may be /etc/vulkan/icd.d on other distros)

Currently AMDVLK v-2019.Q3.5

I do not disable Esync, for me that results in worse performance for this game.

About the only problem I'm having, is that sometimes when I fall and die, when the game reloads performance may get very lagged until I quit the game and restart. But I am pushing my luck with the graphics settings.

title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes:
After renaming 2 files I can start the game with a warning "Attention!, No window adapter installed" and runs fine so far
mv ~/.local/share/Steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/i386/lib/i386-linux-gnu/libcrypto.so.1.0.0 ~/.local/share/Steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/i386/lib/i386-linux-gnu/libcrypto.so.1.0.0_bak

mv ~/.local/share/Steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/amd64/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcrypto.so.1.0.0 ~/.local/share/Steam/ubuntu12_32/steam-runtime/amd64/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcrypto.so.1.0.0_bak
SOURCE: https://steamcommunity.com/app/203160/discussions/0/343787920132866394/
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: The in-game benchmark shows a throughput between 30 and 60fps at 1440p. Disabling Esync is mandatory for the game to run. Feral Game Mode daemon can optimise the use of both CPUs and GPU for a small (but significant) gain in frame rate.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider

notes: Have not run into any issues as of yet. So far running flawless with graphic settings set to Highest at 1920x1080. I get between 80 and 100 FPS depending on where I am at in the environment. Typically, only falling to low 80's when things get really busy.  I'm not sure Feral needs to spend time porting this title with as well as it runs in Proton.  Well done Steam. Keep up the great work.
title: Shadow of the Tomb Raider
